questions.q: In which Indian state is the Basilica of Bom Jesus located?
questions.explanation: The Basilica of Bom Jesus stands in Old Goa, in the state of Goa on India's west coast.

questions.q: The Basilica of Bom Jesus holds the mortal remains of which Catholic saint?
questions.explanation: The basilica enshrines the relics of St Francis Xavier, the Jesuit missionary known as the Apostle of the East.

questions.q: To which Christian religious order is the Basilica of Bom Jesus closely associated?
questions.explanation: The church was built by the Jesuits, the order to which St Francis Xavier himself belonged.

questions.q: Which European colonial power built the Basilica of Bom Jesus?
questions.explanation: Goa was the capital of Portuguese India, and the Portuguese built the basilica in the late 16th century.

questions.q: The Basilica of Bom Jesus is part of which UNESCO World Heritage grouping?
questions.explanation: It was inscribed in 1986 as part of the 'Churches and Convents of Goa' World Heritage Site.

questions.q: What does the name 'Bom Jesus' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Bom Jesus' is Portuguese for 'Good Jesus' or 'Infant Jesus', to whom the basilica is dedicated.

questions.q: What is the religious denomination of the Basilica of Bom Jesus?
questions.explanation: Built by the Portuguese and Jesuits, it is a Roman Catholic church.

questions.q: In which century was the Basilica of Bom Jesus built?
questions.explanation: The church was constructed in the late 16th century, with work completed around 1605.

questions.q: The Basilica of Bom Jesus is located in which historic town in Goa?
questions.explanation: It stands in Old Goa, the former capital of Portuguese India, now a heritage town.

questions.q: St Francis Xavier, whose relics the basilica holds, is often given which title?
options: Apostle of the East / of the Indies
questions.explanation: For his vast missionary travels across Asia, St Francis Xavier is called the Apostle of the East or of the Indies.

questions.q: In what architectural style is the Basilica of Bom Jesus chiefly built?
questions.explanation: The basilica is a celebrated example of Baroque architecture in India.

questions.q: Around what year was construction of the Basilica of Bom Jesus completed?
questions.explanation: Begun in 1594, the church was completed around 1605.

questions.q: A striking feature of the basilica's exterior is that much of its façade lacks which common finish?
questions.explanation: The lime plaster was famously removed in the early 20th century, leaving the red laterite stone of the façade exposed.

questions.q: Where within the basilica are the relics of St Francis Xavier kept?
options: In a silver casket atop an ornate mausoleum
In a wall niche by the entrance
questions.explanation: His remains rest in a silver casket placed on an elaborately carved marble mausoleum in a side chapel.

questions.q: The ornate mausoleum that holds St Francis Xavier's casket was a gift from a ruler of which Italian city?
options: Florence (the Medici / Cosimo III of Tuscany)
questions.explanation: The marble-and-jasper mausoleum was donated by Cosimo III de' Medici, Grand Duke of Tuscany, and crafted by the Florentine sculptor Giovanni Battista Foggini.

questions.q: St Francis Xavier was canonised (declared a saint) in which year?
questions.explanation: He was canonised in 1622 by Pope Gregory XV, together with St Ignatius of Loyola.

questions.q: Roughly how often is the body of St Francis Xavier put on public display in the 'Exposition' at Old Goa?
questions.explanation: The Solemn Exposition of the saint's relics is held roughly once every ten years, drawing huge crowds of pilgrims.

questions.q: The feast day of St Francis Xavier, widely celebrated in Goa, falls on which date?
questions.explanation: His feast is observed on 3 December, the anniversary of his death in 1552.

questions.q: Why is the Basilica of Bom Jesus considered especially significant among Goa's churches?
options: It is the only mosque-turned-church in India
It is the oldest stone church in the world
It holds the relics of St Francis Xavier and is a major Christian pilgrimage site
questions.explanation: Holding the relics of St Francis Xavier, it is one of the most important Christian pilgrimage destinations in Asia.

questions.q: What is the primary building material of the Basilica of Bom Jesus?
questions.explanation: The basilica is built mainly of red laterite stone, the local building material of coastal Goa.

questions.q: The richly gilded main altar of the basilica is dedicated to which figure?
questions.explanation: The grand gilded reredos of the main altar is dedicated to St Ignatius of Loyola, founder of the Jesuits.

questions.q: What title does the church hold that ranks it above an ordinary parish church?
questions.explanation: It bears the title of Minor Basilica, conferred for its historical and spiritual importance.

questions.q: In which year did construction of the Basilica of Bom Jesus begin?
questions.explanation: The foundation work began in 1594, and the church was completed about eleven years later.

questions.q: In which year was the Basilica of Bom Jesus inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site?
questions.explanation: It was inscribed in 1986 as part of the 'Churches and Convents of Goa'.

questions.q: St Francis Xavier died in 1552 on Shangchuan (Sancian) Island while trying to reach which country?
questions.explanation: He died on Sancian Island off the coast of China, awaiting permission to enter the mainland.

questions.q: The Florentine sculptor who created the marble mausoleum for St Francis Xavier was:
questions.explanation: Giovanni Battista Foggini, a Florentine Baroque sculptor, designed the mausoleum commissioned by the Medici.

questions.q: Apart from the main church, what notable institution adjoins the Basilica of Bom Jesus?
options: The Professed House of the Jesuits and an art gallery
questions.explanation: The adjoining Professed House, a Jesuit residence, survives beside the basilica and now houses a modern art gallery.

questions.q: St Francis Xavier was one of the original co-founders, along with St Ignatius of Loyola, of which order in 1540?
questions.explanation: He was among the first companions who founded the Society of Jesus, formally approved in 1540.

questions.q: The interior decoration of the basilica makes lavish use of which technique on its woodwork?
options: Gilding (gold leaf) and carved gilt screens
questions.explanation: The interior is noted for its richly gilded altars and intricately carved gilt woodwork.

questions.q: The relics of St Francis Xavier were brought to Goa from where, having earlier been buried elsewhere after his death?
options: Via Malacca before final reburial in Goa
questions.explanation: After his death his body was first taken to Malacca and then translated to Goa in 1554, where it has remained ever since.
